teamObjective.explanation: With a weak hand of four low clubs and no trumps, the best chance for team 0 is to lead the lowest possible card so that partner (player 2) can win the trick cheaply and take control of the lead.

partnerBelief.summary: Partner (player 2) holds 3 unknown cards. No play has occurred, so partner could have any combination. They are likely to have at least one card that can beat a club 3, and may be able to win this trick and guide subsequent play.

opponentBelief.summary: Opponent player 1 has only 2 cards remaining, indicating they are near finishing and likely hold relatively strong or compact cards. Opponent player 3 has 3 cards and is also unknown. Both opponents may be able to beat low leads, but the near-finishing opponent may be reluctant to spend high cards unless necessary.

actionRationale.primaryReason: Leading the 3 of clubs is the lowest legal lead, minimizing the value opponents need to spend to beat it and maximizing the chance partner can take the trick. It also sheds the weakest card from a hand that cannot control the table.

whyNotAlternatives.reason: The 6 is the highest club and would force a stronger response, but if it wins I would retain weaker cards and lose the chance for partner to lead. If it loses, the opponent gains control from a higher baseline.
The 5 is still a low club, but the 3 is strictly lower and therefore a safer probe; there is no compensating advantage to leading the 5.
The 4 is only marginally higher than the 3 and does not change the strategic situation, while the 3 is the most economical lead.

risks: Opponent player 1 may beat the low lead and go out soon with only 2 cards left.
If partner cannot win the trick, opponents gain tempo at the start of the round.
My remaining clubs are all low, so I may struggle to regain the lead.
riskAssessment.mitigation: By leading the very lowest card, I reduce the chance that an opponent can win cheaply and force them to commit real value if they want control. This preserves my limited remaining cards and gives partner the first opportunity to take the lead.

notes: Opening lead with four weak non-trump clubs; no public signals exist beyond hand counts and the new_game event.
